images.title: Voilier Autonome Instrumenté de Mesures Océanographiques de Surface (VAIMOS) (Ifremer 00555-66697 - 18236).jpg
images.description: The VAIMOS sailing robot - for "Autonomous Instrumented Sailing Boat for Surface Oceanographic Measurements" - uses wind power and solar energy for measurement and observation missions at sea, which can complement the measurement systems currently used. In fact, VAIMOS is programmed to navigate at low cost and autonomously cover an area by carrying out, thanks to its sensors, different types of measurements (temperature, salinity, chlorophyll and turbidity) on the surface of the water (first five centimeters) and at a depth of one meter.

images.title: Blennie dans une rose de mer (Pentapora fascialis) (Ifremer 00378-48881 - 38988).jpg
images.description: Blennies are small bony benthic fish of the Blenniidae family. They are found hidden on the rocky or sandy bottoms of tropical and subtropical waters of the world's seas and oceans. They are particularly abundant in the Red Sea and the Mediterranean. Sea roses ( Pentapora fascialis ), or orange bryozoans, are recognizable by their orange calcareous exoskeleton, which undulates like the petals of a rose. This animal is found along the European coasts, on rocky bottoms swept by currents. These photos were taken in Lanveoc, Brittany.

images.title: Fond à crépidules (Crepidula fornicata) (Ifremer 00424-53573 - 3479).jpg
images.description: Photographed in the harbor of Brest, these slipper limpets, also known as sea slippers, are native to the North-West Atlantic, particularly on the Canadian coasts. Particularly abundant in Brittany and Normandy, they are found all along the eastern Atlantic coast and in the English Channel, resting on sandy and rocky bottoms. This ovoid gastropod, which reaches dimensions of 5cm in length and 2cm in height, is considered an invasive species, shellfish farming activities are an important factor in its proliferation.

images.title: Antiopelle (Antiopella cristata) (Ifremer 00576-68853 - 27755).jpg
images.description:
Nudibranchs are gastropod mollusks, characterized by their lack of a shell, which earned them the name "sea slug".

Antiopella cristata is a nudibranch found in the North Sea, the North-East Atlantic and the Mediterranean. Its body, with a maximum size of 8 cm, is flat, elongated and hidden by numerous outgrowths, the cerata, whose extremities are pale blue. The colour of these animals varies from milky white to light orange. The main sensory organs of nudibranchs are their rhinophores. In these photographs, the rhinophores are clearly visible on the head of this nudibranch. These are lamellate, and their tip is whitish. We can also observe on the head a crest, a caruncle, characteristic of Antiopella cristata .

images.title: Yeux et tentacules d'une coquille Saint-Jacques (Pecten maximus) (Ifremer 00570-68227 - 49129).jpg
images.description: The scallop ( Pecten maximus ) is a bivalve mollusc found in temperate European waters, from Norway to Spain. This photographic series shows its tentacles and eyes: the scallop has the particularity of having up to 200 blue-green eyes. Located on the edge of its mantle, which covers the entire circumference of the mollusc, its eyes measure up to 1 mm in diameter and are built like multiple concave mirrors reflecting light towards the mollusc's retina: they thus allow it to have a very wide field of vision.

images.title: Ophiure noire (Ophiocomina nigra) (Ifremer 00572-68385 - 26254).jpg
images.description: The black brittle star ( Ophiocomina nigra ) is found on various types of substrates. From rocky to sandy-muddy, from the intertidal zone to 200m deep.

images.title: Ophiure noire (Ophiocomina nigra) (Ifremer 00572-68385 - 26255).jpg
images.description: The black brittle star ( Ophiocomina nigra ) is found on various types of substrates. From rocky to sandy-muddy, from the intertidal zone to 200m deep.

images.title: Dispositif d'étude de la croissance des ophiures en rade de Brest (Ifremer 00565-67680 - 41605).jpg
images.description: This series of photographs, taken in Brest harbour as part of a study on the growth of black brittle stars, shows the equipment used and placed on the sandy bottom. Black brittle stars ( Ophiocomina nigra ) are echinoderms found in the North Sea, the English Channel, the Atlantic and the Mediterranean. Many brittle stars are present on the equipment, the surface to be sampled is delimited by quadrats.

images.title: Etoiles de mer et ophiures noires (Ophiocomina nigra) (Ifremer 00417-52885).jpg
images.description:
This photograph shows several species of echinoderms:

- glacial starfish ( Marthasterias glacialis ), located on the left, are recognizable by the numerous conical spines, more or less pointed or blunt, on the arms of the individuals;

- common starfish ( Asterias rubens ) have five arms ending in blunt points and covered with calcareous spines arranged in lines or scattered;

- black brittle stars ( Ophiocomina nigra ) which, as its name suggests, are blackish-brown, or even intensely black.

images.title: Doris orange (Discodoris rosi) (Ifremer 00570-68251).jpg
images.description:
Nudibranchs are gastropod mollusks, characterized by their lack of a shell, which earned them the name "sea slug".

Discodoris rosi is a small nudibranch (maximum 2 cm), found in the tidal zone up to 15 m deep, mainly in the waters of the eastern Atlantic. This species is recognized by its oval shape and its orange mantle dotted with white circles. Its rhinophores (sensory organs) match its coat.

images.title: Bateaux de pêche en rade de Brest (Ifremer 00378-48916 - 35870).jpg
images.description: In Brest harbor, professional fishermen use a wide variety of techniques: potters, gillnetters, longliners and seaweed harvesters.

images.description: In Brest harbor, professional fishermen use a wide variety of techniques: potters, gillnetters, longliners and seaweed harvesters.

images.title: Voilier Autonome Instrumenté de Mesures Océanographiques de Surface (VAIMOS) (Ifremer 00555-66697 - 18231).jpg
images.description: VAIMOS sailing robot and the oceanographic vessel Pourquoi Pas?

images.title: Fraise de mer (Aplidium elegans) et ophiures noires (Ophiocomina nigra) (Ifremer 00737-84921).jpg
images.description: In this photograph, a witness to the biodiversity in the harbor of Brest, we can see some black brittle stars ( Ophiocomina nigra ) approaching a sea strawberry ( Aplidium elegans ), as well as an ascidian with a compact structure and a dominant pink, red or orange color.

images.title: Rose de mer (Pentapora fascialis) (Ifremer 00424-53590 - 3690).jpg
images.description:
Sea rose, photographed here in Brest harbor.

The sea rose is a species of bryozoan found on the coarse rocky bottoms of the Atlantic, the English Channel, the North Sea and the Mediterranean.

Its orange exoskeleton gave it its name of sea rose: it can reach up to 2m in diameter, making it the largest bryozoan in the Atlantic.

images.title: Comptage des pétoncles noirs dans le cadre du projet DIRAPEN (Ifremer 00377-48870 - 200).jpg
images.description:
These photographs show a diver taking samples of black scallops at seedling level. Along a transect, we can observe the presence of quadrats, which allow the sampling of scallops. This sampling will then allow them to be studied according to several criteria: estimation of their number, creation of growth curves, etc. The diver collects the samples in a net.

This series of images was produced as part of the DIRAPEN project : its objective is to promote the return of scallops to Brest harbor and their exploitation, which has fallen from 800 tons produced in the 1980s to 30 tons today. Ifremer is responsible for the ecological monitoring, by diving, of the natural scallop beds and the seeding operations started in 2015.
